APP-CO-4471.summary: The underwriting model declined this 72-month auto-loan application (requested amount $28,400) with a risk score of 0.83 — above the 0.80 decline threshold. The three most influential inputs were debt-to-income ratio (weight 0.34, value 0.46), recent credit inquiries (weight 0.21, six inquiries in the last 90 days), and employment tenure (weight 0.18, value 4 months). The employment-tenure value is worth a second look: the applicant has provided an employer letter showing 18 months of continuous employment — the model may be using a stale source.
reviewer_prompts: Pull the applicant's employer letter and pay stubs from the supporting-docs vault.
Re-run the underwriting model with corrected employment_tenure_months and capture the delta.
If overturning, confirm the corrected tenure is reflected in the consumer disclosure response.

APP-CO-4488.summary: Refinance application declined at risk score 0.78 — within the 'review' band, not auto-declined. Top drivers were debt-to-income ratio (0.43, slightly above policy) and loan-to-value (0.91). The applicant qualifies on credit profile alone but the LTV calculation appears to use an outdated vehicle valuation; re-check the trade-in value before final decision.
reviewer_prompts: Verify the vehicle valuation source date — recent KBB pull or trade-in valuation may shift LTV.
If LTV adjusts to <0.85, model score moves below decline threshold.

APP-CO-4502.summary: Auto-loan application declined at score 0.71. The dominant signal was short employment tenure flagged together with a recent inquiry pattern. Applicant is a 12-year credit-union member with no prior delinquency — the model appears to underweight that membership history.
reviewer_prompts: Member-loyalty override is available — confirm whether it's been applied to this decision.
Document the basis for any override in the audit trail.

CND-NYC-1188.summary: Candidate was screened out at rank 174 of 312 applicants for Senior Risk Analyst. The two most influential signals were tenure pattern (weight 0.32; flagged as 'job-hopping' based on 3 roles in 5 years) and skills overlap (weight 0.27; 62% match to req). The tenure pattern signal is worth a second look: two of the three roles were inside a single 18-month contract engagement that should have been parsed as one continuous tenure.
reviewer_prompts: Open the candidate's resume and confirm whether the 2022–2023 roles were a single contract engagement.
Re-rank with corrected tenure parse and review delta.
If advancing, document the correction and update the candidate-facing review outcome notice.

CND-NYC-1204.summary: Candidate ranked 198 of 287 for Customer Ops Lead. Skills overlap was the dominant driver at 58% match, with an education signal contributing. The candidate's recent customer-ops experience at a comparable company was missed because the role title was non-standard.
reviewer_prompts: Check whether the non-standard role title at prior employer was normalized by Skills-Match.
Consider manual re-skill-tagging.

CND-NYC-1217.summary: Candidate scored 0.62 by the video sentiment model — below the 0.65 advance threshold. Top signals were speech cadence and keyword match. Cadence-based signals are known to disadvantage non-native English speakers and candidates with speech differences; manual review recommended before relying on this score.
reviewer_prompts: Review the video manually; do not rely on the cadence signal in isolation.
Check whether IL BIPA consent was captured before the video was recorded — this affects whether the recording can be retained at all.

BUY-CA-7733.summary: Pinetop MAC declined this 60-month captive-finance application for a 2026 Aria EV ($48,900) at a risk score of 0.81 — narrowly above the 0.78 decline threshold. The three most influential inputs were debt-to-income ratio (weight 0.31, value 0.44), credit bureau score (weight 0.24, mid-680s band), and — critically — a telematics-derived 'behavioral risk' signal (weight 0.18) drawn from the applicant's prior connected vehicle. That telematics signal is the data point worth examining first: the applicant's connected-vehicle activation flow does not appear to have captured a secondary-use opt-in for use of driving data in financing decisions. If the signal was used without a valid opt-in, the model should be re-scored without it.
reviewer_prompts: Open audit entry ae-a-003 to confirm the data lineage — did telematics features get joined to the financing feature vector?
Pull the consumer's connected-vehicle activation record and check whether a secondary-use opt-in for financing was captured. If not, telematics_risk_signal must be removed from this decision.
Re-score without telematics_risk_signal; if the new score falls below 0.78, document the override and escalate the upstream policy gap to the Privacy team.

BUY-CA-7741.summary: Captive-finance application for a 2024 CPO Sentra was declined at risk score 0.74. Dominant inputs were debt-to-income ratio (0.46) and loan-to-value (0.93). The applicant qualifies on credit profile alone but the LTV uses a stale CPO valuation; reconfirming the valuation could shift the LTV below 0.85 and the score below threshold.
reviewer_prompts: Verify the CPO vehicle valuation source date; a current KBB pull may shift LTV.
Confirm no telematics-derived features were used (this is a new-to-Pinetop applicant; backfill defaults expected).

BUY-CA-7758.summary: Trade-in appraisal came in 18% below KBB book value on a 2021 Pinetop Altima-equivalent. The dominant downward signal was a 'behavioral wear' adjustment derived from telematics — harsh-braking rate and high-acceleration cohort assignment over the 12 months prior to trade-in. The KBB book itself was within market norms. Review the telematics adjustment first: the magnitude (-18%) is well outside the model's documented confidence band for this signal.
reviewer_prompts: Pull KBB AI Valuation packet §3.2 documented confidence band for the telematics-wear signal; -18% appears out of band.
Check whether the consumer opted into telematics-derived trade-in adjustments at activation.
If override warranted, re-run valuation without the wear signal and document the basis.
